severity_rationale: Missing PKCE on first-party public-client OAuth flow. No direct exploit, but deviates from OAuth 2.1 / RFC 8252 best practice and amplifies any future code-interception vulnerability. Standalone severity is P4/P5; elevated to P4 because the client_id=store flow is the exact surface exercised by the F-A-001 chain.
validation_method: Curl: sent code_challenge; flow completed without any code_verifier. Sending no code_challenge also completed successfully — PKCE not enforced.
response_summary: PKCE is neither required nor meaningfully processed. Authorize flow completes identically whether code_challenge is present or absent. No rejection for missing PKCE on public-client-style first-party flow.
impact: Auth-code interception scenarios (network MITM, XSS-on-callback, Referer/log leak) allow code replay without code_verifier.
chain: Amplifies F-A-001 (Referer leak becomes code replay).
remediation: Enforce PKCE (S256) for public first-party clients per OAuth 2.1.
